scope_note: QA Fibonacci phi-Slope Formula cert [421]. Primary sources: Hensel, K. (1897) 'Ueber eine neue Begruendung der Theorie der algebraischen Zahlen' Jahresbericht der DMV 6 pp. 83-88 (Hensel lifting / Newton iteration for p-adic roots); Wall, D.D. (1960) 'Fibonacci series modulo m' doi:10.2307/2309169 (rank of apparition alpha(p)); Sun, Z.-H. and Sun, Z.-W. (1992) 'Fibonacci and Lucas congruences and their applications' Acta Arithmetica 61 pp. 119-129 (p^2-level Fibonacci structure and Wall-Sun-Sun definition). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): For a split prime p (i.e. (5/p)=+1, p!=2,5), Binet's formula lifts from F_p to Z/p^2 Z via Hensel's lemma. Specifically: (SETUP) let s5 = canonical sqrt(5) mod p (smaller of the two roots); let s_tilde = s5 + p*t in Z/p^2 Z where t = -(s5^2-5)/p * (2*s5)^{-1} mod p (Newton step); let phi_tilde = (1+s_tilde)/2 and psi_tilde = (1-s_tilde)/2 in Z/p^2 Z (both exist since 2 is invertible mod p^2 for odd p). (ALGEBRAIC IDENTITIES) phi_tilde^2 = phi_tilde+1, psi_tilde^2 = psi_tilde+1, phi_tilde+psi_tilde=1, phi_tilde*psi_tilde=-1 (all mod p^2) — these follow from s_tilde^2=5 and the quadratic formula. (BINET MOD p^2) F_n = (phi_tilde^n - psi_tilde^n) * s_tilde^{-1} mod p^2 for all n >= 0: follows because the right-hand side satisfies the Fibonacci recurrence a_{n+1}=a_n+a_{n-1} (by linearity and phi^2=phi+1, psi^2=psi+1) with a_0=0 and a_1=1. Non-trivial: proves the QA T-step orbit (the integer Fibonacci recurrence mod p^2) is EXACTLY the Binet product in Z/p^2 Z; the two computation paths are identical. (PHI-SLOPE FORMULA) delta(p) = (phi_tilde^{alpha(p)} - psi_tilde^{alpha(p)}) * s_tilde^{-1} / p mod p: the Fibonacci depth invariant (cert [419]) is the normalised difference of the two lifted golden-ratio branches at the first zero. Geometric meaning: delta(p) measures how far the two Hensel lifts of phi diverge at depth p^2 after alpha(p) T-steps. (WSS REFORMULATION) delta(p)=0 iff phi_tilde^{alpha(p)} = psi_tilde^{alpha(p)} mod p^2: WSS prime (cert [420]) iff the two lifted branches are indistinguishable at depth p^2. For inert primes (5/p)=-1: phi, psi live in F_{p^2} but not F_p; the Binet formula still holds over Z/p^2 Z[sqrt 5] (the unramified degree-2 extension); the phi-slope formula generalises but uses Z[phi]/p^2 arithmetic; cert [421] covers only the split case where all computations are in Z/p^2 Z directly. CHECKS: (C1) HENSEL CORRECTNESS: for 20 split primes {11,19,29,31,41,59,61,71,79,89,101,109,131,139,149,151,179,181,191,199}: s_tilde^2=5 mod p^2, phi_tilde^2=phi_tilde+1 mod p^2, psi_tilde^2=psi_tilde+1 mod p^2, phi_tilde*psi_tilde=-1 mod p^2, phi_tilde+psi_tilde=1 mod p^2; all 20 PASS; falsifiable: any p where Newton step fails to give s_tilde^2=5 mod p^2 refutes; (C2) BINET FORMULA MOD p^2: 225 cases (n=1..15, 15 split primes in [7,150]): F_n = (phi_tilde^n-psi_tilde^n)/s_tilde mod p^2 matches fib_fast(n, p^2) for all; falsifiable: any (p,n) where Binet disagrees with recurrence refutes; (C3) DELTA FROM BINET = DELTA FROM RECURRENCE: for all 45 split primes p<=500: delta(p) from (phi^alpha-psi^alpha)/s_tilde/p mod p matches delta(p) from fib_fast(alpha,p^2)//p%p; all 45/45 PASS; (C4) WSS VIA PHI-SLOPE: for all 45 split primes p<=500: (phi_tilde^alpha == psi_tilde^alpha mod p^2) iff (F_alpha == 0 mod p^2); both conditions are False for every prime (no WSS prime found among split primes <=500); falsifiable: any split prime where the two conditions disagree refutes. LANGLANDS NOTE: phi_tilde is a p-adic unit in Z_p^x (for split p, Z_p = p-adic integers where 5 has a square root); its orbit under T (multiplication by phi in Z_p^x) has order alpha(p) at first return. The equidistribution of delta(p)/p over split primes follows from Hecke equidistribution for the Hecke character on Q(sqrt 5) associated to the Frobenius at p. This provides the algebraic underpinning for the WSS heuristic O(log log X) from cert [420]. CHAIN: [416] alpha(p)|p-(5/p) -> [417] delta(p)=F_alpha/p mod p -> [418] Cassini parity -> [419] delta=1 census -> [420] delta=0 is WSS -> [421] delta(p) is phi-slope in Z/p^2 Z for split primes. All integer arithmetic, no float, Theorem NT. Derived 2026-06-14.
